observations.recommendation:
Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity is very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities.
Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
We recommended that Management require the official responsible for accepting deliveries to comply with the requirements of COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
The management shall see to it that all developmental projects were duly verified/inspected by the COA Inspectorate Team.

We recommended that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended that Management should adhere with stated COA laws and regulations whereby cash advances granted to Municipal Treasurer shall be utilized for legally authorized specific purpose and payment of expenditures for various projects shall be paid directly to the suppliers.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all collections be
deposited intact with the nearest authorized depository bank within the
reglamentary period as required under Sec. 32, Vol. I, of COA Circular No. 2002-
003 dated June 20, 2002, known as New Government Accounting System for LGU
dated January 1, 2002, in order to safeguard government funds from possible
misappropriation and theft.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that management strictly observe adherence to Section 74 of PD
1445, and Section 310 of RA 7160 and Rule XXXVI, Article 450 of its IRR; (ii)
prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements on all funds monthly to the
auditor for verification; and, (iii) maintain separate books and depository accounts
for General Fund and Trust Fund in order to easily identify and establish the
correct balances of each agency fund per cashbooks that shall correspond with the
balances of cash in bank per bank reconciliation statements for the audit period.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that management require the official responsible for accepting
deliveries to comply with the requirements of COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 95-
006 dated May 18, 1995.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that Management should adhere with stated COA laws and
regulations whereby cash advances granted to Municipal Treasurer shall be
utilized for legally authorized specific purpose and payment of expenditures for
various projects shall be paid directly to the suppliers.

RECOMMENDATION
Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this
activity is very important in checking actual existence of the assets and
determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial
responsibilities.
Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on
PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately
thereafter. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment
Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and
likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and
accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend requiring the Municipal Accountant and other Accountable
Officers concerned to submit the required financial and other reports with all its
supporting documents to the auditor in charge with the prescribed period.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that the management shall see to it that all developmental projects
were duly verified/inspected by the COA Inspectorate Team.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in
COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of
Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote
Good Governance through transparency and accountability.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommend that, management should comply with the guidelines set forth
under DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 in the appropriation
and implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities by
organizing a GAD Focal Point, developing a 3-year GAD plan, appropriating
at least five percent (5%) for GAD-related programs, projects and activities
from its annual and supplemental budget and implementing GAD related
program, projects and activities in line with its GAD Plan.

We recommend that the management should require the Accountable Officer
concerned to render their reports as required in Section 15 and Section 37 of
COA Circular 92-382.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials of the LGU to support all claims with sufficient and required documentations, particularly, on the utilization of the COVID19 appropriated funds in their procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.

We recommend that proper internal control over supplies or property be adopted and it demands that, proper and accurate recording as to its valuation, existence, ownership and completeness shall be kept for all incoming and out-going supplies or property in pursuant to Sec. 42, 45 to 46 of NGAS Manual Vol. I

We recommended that Management strictly observe adherence to the provisions of Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002; (i) the MPDO prepares Annual Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and annual work and financial plan where disbursements of LDRRMF is strictly based; (ii) transfer to a Special Trust Fund the unexpended LDRRMF for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) within the next five year.

We recommended that, management should comply with the guidelines set forth under DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 in the appropriation and implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities by organizing a GAD Focal Point, developing a 3-year GAD plan, appropriating at least five percent (5%) for GAD-related programs, projects and activities from its annual and supplemental budget and implementing GAD related program, projects and activities in line with its GAD Plan.

We recommended requiring the Municipal Accountant and other Accountable Officers concerned to submit the required financial and other reports with all its supporting documents to the auditor in charge with the prescribed period.
We recommended that the management to support all claims with the required documentations prior to release of payments / checks to validate disbursements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials of
the PGO-Sulu to support all claims with sufficient and required documentations,
particularly, on the utilization of the COVID19 appropriated funds in their
procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must

required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an
alternative mode of procurement.

We recommend that the management direct the concerned accountant to observe
the perpetual system in recording purchases and to use the RSMI in recording
issuances and consumption of stocks and prepare adjusting entries for all noted
errors in recording to correct the balances of inventory accounts.

complete listings of completed and on-going projects funded out of the 20%
Local Development Fund for CY 2018, the letter request for inspection signed

001 necessary for the review/evaluation and ocular inspection of the
development projects

Recommendation
We  recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of  COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or public  notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office.

Recommendation

Recommendation
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all collections be deposited intact with the nearest authorized depository bank within the reglamentary period as required under Sec. 32, Vol. I, of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002, known as New Government Accounting System for LGU  dated January 1, 2002, in order to safeguard government funds from possible misappropriation and theft.

Recommendation
We recommend that management particularly to the members of the Bids and Award Committee, to strictly comply with the aforesaid regulations.  That in cases of procurement method applied is not within the approved APP, the BAC through a resolution must recommend to the head of the procuring agency the specific alternative method of procurement to be used and secure the prior approval, before resorting to any alternative method of procurement pursuant to Sec. 48.1 and 48.3 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.

Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

Recommendation
We recommend requiring the Municipal Accountant and other Accountable Officers concerned to submit the required financial and other reports with all its supporting documents to the auditor in charge with the prescribed period.

Recommendation
We recommend that the management should require the Accountable Officer concerned to render their reports as required in Section 15 and Section 37 of COA Circular 92-382.

Recommendation
We  recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of  COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or public  notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office.

We recommend that the management should adhere with Section 12 of No. R.A. 9003 which states “that each city or municipality shall form a city or municipal waste management board that shall prepare, submit and implement a plan for the safe and sanitary management of solid waste generated in areas under its geographic and political coverage”.
